domain: Haudenosaunee Condolence Ceremony (Iroquois Confederacy; 50-peg Condolence Cane mnemonic registry of confederacy chief titles; fixed 3-stage Dekanawida-Hiawatha ritual: dry-eyes / open-ears / clear-throat; two-moiety peer-led recovery)
specific_mechanism: Multi-agent state-recovery protocol triggered by heartbeat-loss: peers reference shared role-anchor registry then execute fixed 3-stage ritual (invalidate-stale-cache / reload-role-context / restore-output-channel).
llm_application: CONDOLENCE-PROTOCOL: a multi-agent state-recovery protocol. When agent A_i's heartbeat is lost, two peer agents A_{i-1} and A_{i+1} (the 'two moieties') execute a deterministic 3-stage ritual referencing the SHARED ROLE-ANCHOR REGISTRY (the 'cane'): STAGE-1 DRY-EYES = invalidate stale shared cache entries owned by A_i; STAGE-2 OPEN-EARS = reload A_i's role-context from registry into a fresh agent instance; STAGE-3 CLEAR-THROAT = send probe-ack to re-establish A_i's output channel. Recovery sequence ATOMICITY enforced — all 3 stages must complete before traffic resumes. Differs from Raft/Paxos failover (no role-anchor semantics, leader-election based), TraceFix (formal verification not recovery), Mesh Memory Protocol (role anchors but no fixed 3-stage atomic ritual) by combining (a) fixed-sequence atomic recovery ritual, (b) shared role-anchor registry, (c) two-moiety peer-driven execution.
novelty_claim: TraceFix coordination repair, MAST failure taxonomy, Mesh Memory Protocol role anchors, Raft/Paxos failover already exist. CONDOLENCE-PROTOCOL recombines fixed-3-stage atomic ritual + role-anchor cane + two-moiety executor. Recombination of existing components.
